title: when to use futur anterieur, and provide example

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: when to use futur anterieur, and provide example

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
Futur antérieur is used to describe an action that will have been completed in the future before another action takes place.

Example: 
Je serai parti avant que tu arrives.
(I will have left before you arrive.)

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: how would one apply for FBLA?

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
To apply for the Future Business Leaders of America (FBLA), you typically need to follow these steps:

1. Find a chapter: Locate a local FBLA chapter in your school or community. If there is no existing chapter, you can start your own by contacting FBLA-PBL National Center for more information.

2. Join the chapter: Once you have found a chapter, you will need to become a member. Contact the chapter adviser or officers to learn about the membership requirements and dues that you need to pay.

3. Participate in activities: Attend meetings and events hosted by your chapter to get involved in your local FBLA community. Participating in these activities can help you develop your skills, build your network, and prepare for competitions.

4. Register for competitions: FBLA offers a variety of competitive events in areas such as finance, management, marketing, and technology. You can register through your chapter to participate in these events and gain recognition for your skills.

By following these steps, you can become a member of FBLA and take advantage of the opportunities it provides to develop your leadership and business skills.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: who do i contact at FBLA-PBL for more information on how to start a chapter?

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
To get more information on how to start a chapter of Future Business Leaders of America-Phi Beta Lambda (FBLA-PBL), you can contact the organization's National Center. Here are the steps:

1. Visit the FBLA-PBL website at www.fbla-pbl.org.

2. Click on the "Contact Us" link at the bottom of the page.

3. Select "Starting a New Chapter" from the dropdown menu.

4. Fill out the form with your contact details and a brief message explaining your inquiry regarding starting a new chapter.

5. Submit the form, and someone from the FBLA-PBL National Center should get back to you with more information.

Alternatively, you can contact the FBLA-PBL National Center directly through the following contact information:

Future Business Leaders of America-Phi Beta Lambda, Inc.
1912 Association Drive
Reston, VA 20191-1591
Phone: (800) FBLA-WIN or (800) 325-2946
Email: <PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_EMAIL_ADDRESS>

By contacting FBLA-PBL, you can get the information you need to start a new chapter and become a part of a great organization for developing business and leadership skills.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: there is no dropdown menu when i click "contact us"at the bottom

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
I apologize for the confusion. You can also directly visit the "Starting a New Chapter" page by typing in the URL: https://www.fbla-pbl.org/charters/start-a-new-chapter/. 

On this page, you should be able to find information about starting a new chapter of FBLA-PBL, including the required steps, resources, and contact information. 

If you have any further questions or concerns, you can contact FBLA-PBL through their National Center using the following contact details:

Future Business Leaders of America-Phi Beta Lambda, Inc.
1912 Association Drive
Reston, VA 20191-1591
Phone: (800) FBLA-WIN or (800) 325-2946
Email: <PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_EMAIL_ADDRESS>

I hope this helps!
